chunks.text: Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR) at EECS, UC Berkeley, focuses on modeling systems and machines to respond appropriately to inputs, using optimization and mathematical techniques. Applications range from semiconductor process control to hybrid, networked, nonlinear, and learning control, with interdisciplinary collaborations. Robotics includes mobile autonomous systems from millimeter-sized robots to large helicopters, fixed systems for assembly, human augmentation like telepresence and virtual reality, and key areas in image understanding and computer vision to assist humans.
chunks.question: What is the focus of Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics at EECS?
chunks.answer: Modeling systems and machines for appropriate responses.

chunks.text: Topics in CIR include biological systems such as bio-mimetic robotics, neuro-prosthetic systems, computational motor control, human-centered control, man-machine systems, computer vision, active and real-time perception, neuroengineering, biological systems modeling, and systems biology.
chunks.question: What are some biological system topics in CIR?

chunks.text: Research centers in CIR include Berkeley Air & Space Center, Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab, Berkeley Center for Computational Imaging, Berkeley Deep Drive, Berkeley Equity and Access in Algorithms, Mechanisms, and Optimization, Berkeley Laboratory for Information and System Sciences, Biomimetic Millisystems Lab, Center for Augmented Cognition, Center for Automation and Learning for Medical Robotics, Center for Neural Engineering & Prostheses, Center for the Theoretical Foundations of Learning, Inference, Information, Intelligence, Mathematics and Microeconomics at Berkeley, CITRIS Health, CITRIS Sustainable Infrastructure, Human-Assistive Robotic Technologies Lab, Industrial Engineering and Operations Research, Laboratory for Automation Science and Engineering, Verified Human Interfaces, Control, and Learning for Semi-Autonomous Systems, and Video and Image Processing Lab.
chunks.question: Name a research center in CIR.

chunks.text: Primary faculty in CIR include Pieter Abbeel, Murat Arcak, Alexandre Bayen, Anca Dragan, Ronald S. Fearing, Ken Goldberg, Jiantao Jiao, Giuseppe Loianno, Michael Lustig, Pierluigi Nuzzo, Gireeja Ranade, Benjamin Recht, S. Shankar Sastry, Somayeh Sojoudi, and Claire Tomlin (coordinator).
chunks.question: Who is the coordinator of primary faculty in CIR?

chunks.text: Secondary faculty in CIR include Venkat Anantharam, Ruzena Bajcsy, Peter Bartlett, Thomas F. Budinger, John F. Canny, Leon O. Chua, Thomas Courtade, Trevor Darrell, Laurent El Ghaoui, Michael Jordan, Sergey Levine, Jitendra Malik, Song Mei, Kristofer Pister, Elijah Polak, Kameshwar Poolla, Kannan Ramchandran, Jaijeet Roychowdhury, Stuart J. Russell, Anant Sahai, Seth R. Sanders, Alberto L. Sangiovanni-Vincentelli, Sanjit A. Seshia, Grigory Tikhomirov, Stella Yu, and Avideh Zakhor.
chunks.question: Name a secondary faculty member in CIR.

chunks.text: Faculty awards in CIR include ACM Prize in Computing for Pieter Abbeel (2021), MacArthur Fellow for Claire Tomlin (2006), National Academy of Sciences members like Jitendra Malik (2015) and Michael Jordan (2010), National Academy of Engineering members such as Ken Goldberg (2026) and Stuart J. Russell (2025), American Academy of Arts and Sciences members including Alberto L. Sangiovanni-Vincentelli (2024), Berkeley Citation for Ruzena Bajcsy (2023), UC Berkeley Distinguished Teaching Award for Kameshwar Poolla (1996), and Sloan Research Fellows like Song Mei (2025) and Sergey Levine (2019).
chunks.question: Who won the ACM Prize in Computing in 2021?

chunks.text: Related courses in CIR include CS 287 (Advanced Robotics), EECS 127 (Optimization Models in Engineering), EECS 227AT (Optimization Models in Engineering), EE C106A (Introduction to Robotics), EE C106B (Robotic Manipulation and Interaction), EE 120 (Signals and Systems), EE C128 (Feedback Control Systems), EE 192 (Mechatronic Design Laboratory), and EE 221A (Linear System Theory).
chunks.question: What is the course code for Introduction to Robotics?
